commonProblems.h2: Mini Convertible Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mini Convertible relies heavily on the N14/N18 turbo petrol, N47/B47 diesel, and W11 supercharged engines, all of which dominate UK replacement demand due to well-documented weak points. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components specifically designed to address the original Mini Convertible failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. The decision tool below compares genuine UK repair costs against full replacement so you can make an informed, value-driven choice before committing to garage labour.

problems.group: Mini Convertible N14 Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Mini Convertible N14 Timing Chain Failure - Why Early R57 Petrol Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Mini Convertible Cooper S (R57, 2007-2010, N14B16A & N14B16C)
problems.rootCause: The N14B16A uses a rear-mounted timing chain routed behind the engine block. Inadequate oil flow to the hydraulic tensioner causes premature guide wear, chain stretch, and the notorious "death rattle" that precedes catastrophic valve-train failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removed, original chain, guides, and tensioner replaced with reinforced components.
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable if caught before noise develops. Adds ~60-80k miles to engine life.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Mini Convertible N14 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Failed N14 unit swapped for a factory-spec rebuild with upgraded tensioner, guides, and bench-tested compression. Includes supply & fit and 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores like-new reliability. Eliminates chain anxiety permanently.

problems.recommendation: If your Mini Convertible N14 is still quiet and below 60k miles, a preventative chain kit is the most cost-effective path. However, once rattling starts on cold starts, internal damage to guides and tensioner mounts is usually already present. At that stage, investing in a rebuilt N14 engine with upgraded timing components is significantly more reliable and often cheaper than paying dealer labour for a second repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt N14 Mini Convertible engine prices for your exact variant

problems.group: Mini Convertible N47 Diesel Timing Chain & Tensioner Wear
problems.h4: Mini Convertible N47 Diesel Timing Chain & Tensioner Wear - Why Cooper D Models Snap Under Load
problems.affectedModels: Mini Convertible Cooper D & Cooper SD (R57, 2009-2015, N47D16 & N47D20)
problems.rootCause: Shared architecture with BMW diesel platforms places the timing chain against the bulkhead. Restricted lubrication flow and aggressive tensioner geometry cause accelerated chain stretch. In high-mileage Mini Convertible diesel applications, the chain often skips teeth or snaps entirely, destroying the valvetrain.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine out service replacing chain rails, tensioners, and oil pump drive sprocket.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if the chain hasn't jumped timing. Does not address underlying oil flow limitation.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Mini Convertible N47/B47 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Catastrophic failure N47 replaced with rebuilt unit featuring reinforced tensioners, improved oil pickup, and EGR/DPF compliance. Supply & fit included.
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for vehicles over 85k miles or post-snap failure. Eliminates chain weakness.

problems.recommendation: Mini Convertible N47 owners should treat any rattling as a critical warning. Unlike petrol engines, diesel chains under load can snap with little prior notice, often bending valves or cracking the cylinder head. If your Convertible has already suffered a snap, or is approaching 100k miles with chain noise, a rebuilt replacement with upgraded tensioner geometry is the only financially sound long-term solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt N47 Mini Convertible diesel engine quotes today

problems.group: Mini Convertible High Pressure Fuel Pump & Turbocharger Failure
problems.h4: Mini Convertible High Pressure Fuel Pump & Turbocharger Failure - Direct Injection Turbo Fatigue
problems.affectedModels: Mini Convertible Cooper S & John Cooper Works (R57, 2007-2015, N14B16A, N18B16A, N14B16C)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection HPFP seals degrade under repeated heat cycles, dropping rail pressure and causing lean running conditions. The resulting combustion temperature spikes starve the turbocharger bearings of proper oil pressure, accelerating shaft wear and eventual seal failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New OEM-spec fuel pump, replacement turbocharger assembly, intercooler cleaning, and ECU adaptation.
repairOptions.longevity: Fixes immediate symptoms but does not address carbon build-up or underlying block wear from lean running.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Mini Convertible Turbo Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete N14/N18/B48 rebuild with upgraded HPFP, reinforced turbo oil feed lines, walnut-blasted intake, and full dyno validation. Supply & fit with 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Ideal for engines showing blue smoke, metal shavings in oil, or persistent boost faults.

problems.recommendation: If the HPFP fails but the turbo survives, replacing both units independently can be cost-effective on lower-mileage Mini Convertible examples. However, if the engine has been running lean for extended periods, piston ring wear and cylinder scoring are common. A full rebuilt unit with upgraded fuel system components and cleaned intake ports consistently outperforms piecemeal turbo/HPFP swaps in longevity.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Mini Convertible turbo engine replacement costs

problems.group: Mini Convertible W11 Supercharger Coupling & Oil Feed Degradation
problems.h4: Mini Convertible W11 Supercharger Coupling & Oil Feed Degradation - Early R52 Weak Points
problems.rootCause: The W11 uses a roots-type supercharger driven by a rubber-coupled isolator pulley. Over time, heat exposure hardens the coupling, causing violent rattling under acceleration. Simultaneously, the rubber supercharger oil feed line degrades, restricting lubrication to the snout bearings.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Coupling & Oil Line Upgrade
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supercharger isolator replaced with reinforced polyurethane version, hard-line oil feed installed, and system flushed.
repairOptions.longevity: Highly recommended at 50k miles or first sign of belt-side rattle. Extends supercharger life by 40k+ miles.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Mini Convertible W11 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: W11 swap with rebuilt long block, new supercharger assembly, upgraded timing components, and full ancillary refresh. Supply & fit with 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary if oil starvation has seized supercharger bearings or damaged internal crank journals.

problems.recommendation: The W11 supercharger coupling is one of the most predictable failures in the Mini Convertible lineage. If your R52 rattles under load but still pulls strongly, upgrade the isolator and oil feed immediately. This preventative fix costs a fraction of a dealer supercharger replacement and preserves the original engine. Only consider a full rebuilt W11 if bearing seizure or internal crank damage has already occurred.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Mini Convertible R52 engine prices for your variant

problems.group: Mini Convertible B48 Cooling System & Carbon Build-Up
problems.h4: Mini Convertible B48 Cooling System & Carbon Build-Up - Modern F57 Direct Injection Challenges
problems.rootCause: The B48A20A relies on an electric thermostat and plastic coolant housing that frequently crack under thermal cycling. Coolant loss causes localized overheating, while direct injection promotes severe carbon deposits on intake valves, restricting airflow and increasing combustion temperatures.

repairOptions.tier: Preventative - Thermostat Housing + Walnut Blast Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cracked plastic housing replaced with alloy unit, intake valves walnut-blasted, thermostat calibrated, and cooling system pressure-tested.
repairOptions.longevity: Essential maintenance at 60k miles. Prevents overheating damage and restores power. Not a repair for warped blocks.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Mini Convertible B48 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete B48 rebuild with alloy cooling components, cleaned/ported heads, reinforced timing chain, and full bench testing. Supply & fit with 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if overheating caused warped cylinder head, cracked block, or severe ringland damage.

problems.recommendation: Modern Mini Convertible B48 engines are highly durable if the cooling system is addressed proactively. Replace the plastic thermostat housing at the first sign of weeping, and schedule intake walnut blasting every 40k miles. If the Convertible has already suffered severe overheating with coolant mixing into oil, internal metal fatigue is likely. A rebuilt B48 with reinforced cooling pathways is the safest, most warranty-backed solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Mini Convertible B48 engine prices and specialists

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mini Convertible
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing an N14 timing chain rattle, an N47 diesel tensioner fault, or a W11 supercharger leak, Mini Convertible engine failures follow predictable patterns that can quickly escalate into write-off territory. EngineMarket removes the guesswork by letting you compare live prices from trusted UK Mini Convertible specialists who supply fully rebuilt engines with upgraded internal components designed to permanently fix the original weak point. With nationwide delivery, transparent supply & fit options, and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty on every unit, you can secure a reliable replacement without paying main dealer premiums or risking unreliable used units.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mini Convertible Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mini Convertible and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Mini Convertible Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every condition is available through our UK specialist network with typical price ranges based on your exact Mini Convertible variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Mini Convertible Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Carefully stripped and inspected units with worn seals, gaskets and timing components replaced to match factory specifications.
types.frontDescription: Carefully stripped and inspected units with worn seals, gaskets and timing components replaced to match factory specifications.
types.frontDisclaimer: Covers all N14, N47 and B48 codes with full compatibility verification.
types.backDescription: These units undergo partial disassembly to address known wear points like plastic timing guides and valve cover leaks. Each Mini Convertible block is pressure tested, fitted with upgraded tensioners where applicable, and matched to your R57 or F57 chassis specifications before dispatch.
backBullets: Replaced timing chains, guides and hydraulic tensioners to resolve known N14 rattling faults.

engineSizes.h2: Mini Convertible Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement officially fitted to the UK Mini Convertible range is detailed below, with compatible engine codes, generation years and common failure notes to ensure accurate replacement matching.

items.title: Mini Convertible 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: Introduced during the mid-life R57 generation, the compact 1.6-litre unit served as the efficient entry point for diesel buyers seeking lower running costs without sacrificing the model's signature agile handling. Built with a reinforced aluminium block and common-rail injection, this displacement quickly became the standard choice for urban Convertible drivers prioritising fuel economy over outright torque.
commonFailurePoints: EGR valve clogging at higher mileage
items.cta: Get quotes for Mini Convertible 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Mini Convertible 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: Spanning both the R57 and current F57 generations, the 2.0-litre diesel forms the torque-rich backbone of the Convertible's long-distance touring lineup, delivering strong mid-range pull ideal for the heavier soft-top chassis. Early units utilised the N47 architecture before transitioning to the refined B47 block, which incorporated improved cooling pathways, stricter emissions compliance and updated turbocharger bearings for enhanced durability.
commonFailurePoints: AdBlue injector faults on later F57 models
items.cta: Get quotes for Mini Convertible 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Mini Convertible 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Debuted with the launch of the F57 chassis in 2016, the 1.5-litre three-cylinder replaced older naturally aspirated units as the new base-spec powerplant for modern Convertible buyers. Featuring TwinPower turbocharging, a balance shaft to smooth out inline-three vibrations and direct fuel injection, this compact displacement delivers responsive low-end torque while maintaining excellent urban efficiency and reduced emissions.
items.cta: Compare Mini Convertible 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mini Convertible 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: Dominating the R52 and R57 generations for over a decade, the 1.6-litre petrol covers everything from Chrysler-sourced naturally aspirated base blocks to heavily modified twin-scroll turbo performance variants. This versatile displacement introduced BMW’s Valvetronic technology and direct injection to the Convertible lineup, fundamentally shifting the brand’s performance character while generating the highest volume of UK replacement enquiries due to well-documented timing and lubrication weaknesses.
commonFailurePoints: Rear timing chain tensioner failure on N14/N18 turbos
supercharger isolator wear on W11 units
items.cta: Compare Mini Convertible 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mini Convertible 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Exclusive to the current F57 generation, the 2.0-litre turbocharged inline-four serves as the high-performance flagship for both Cooper S and John Cooper Works Convertible trims, delivering brisk acceleration and refined highway cruising. Incorporating an integrated exhaust manifold, twin-vane turbocharger and advanced thermal management, this displacement is engineered for sustained high-load operation but requires careful maintenance to manage direct-injection carbon accumulation and high-pressure fuel system stress.
items.cta: Compare Mini Convertible 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mini Convertible Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel Mini Convertibles span the R57 and F57 generations, with the B47 2.0L now dominant in UK replacement enquiries due to its refined efficiency and improved timing chain design over the earlier N47.
items.descriptor: Diesel Mini Convertibles span the R57 and F57 generations, with the B47 2.0L now dominant in UK replacement enquiries due to its refined efficiency and improved timing chain design over the earlier N47.

B47D20A - 2.0L diesel (F57), refined successor with updated cooling and emissions

items.description: Petrol powers every Mini Convertible generation, from the Chrysler-sourced W10/W11 units in the R52 through the problematic N14/N18 turbos in the R57 to the modern B38/B48 engines in the current F57.
items.descriptor: Petrol powers every Mini Convertible generation, from the Chrysler-sourced W10/W11 units in the R52 through the problematic N14/N18 turbos in the R57 to the modern B38/B48 engines in the current F57.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mini Convertible Petrol Engine Families

N14/N18 timing chain tensioner failures driving high replacement volume
Direct injection carbon build-up requiring walnut blasting maintenance

items.description: No official hybrid, plug-in hybrid or pure electric Mini Convertible variants were sold in the UK market; all replacements focus on conventional petrol and diesel combustion engines.

engineYears.h2: Mini Convertible Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers all major Mini Convertible engine changes from launch to present, organised by year so owners can quickly identify their exact unit and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original R52 Convertible debuted with Chrysler-sourced W10/W11 1.6L engines, establishing the signature go-kart handling that defined early UK ownership.
keyChanges: First-generation drop-top Mini launches on the R50 platform with soft-top mechanism
Supercharged W11 replaces older Tritec architecture for Cooper S performance trim

years.preview: 2008 final model years introduce Checkmate, Highgate and Park Lane limited editions before R57 transition
mainEngines: W11B16A - 1.6L Supercharged Petrol (Cooper S, John Cooper Works)
knownFor: Iconic supercharger whine and sharp steering response deliver classic Mini driving dynamics
W11 supercharger rubber isolator coupling hardens after 50-70k miles causing belt-side rattling under load
Naturally aspirated W10 is highly reliable but suffers from common valve cover gasket and oil filter housing leaks
Strong UK enthusiast demand for period-correct rebuilt units with upgraded couplers as standard
years.cta: Compare rebuilt R52 Mini Convertible engine prices

years.description: The R57 generation arrived with BMW’s new N47 diesel and N14 turbo petrol, bringing higher efficiency but introducing the infamous rear-mounted timing chain architecture that dominates UK replacement demand.
keyChanges: Complete chassis refresh with updated R57 platform codes and improved soft-top weather sealing
N47 1.6L/2.0L diesel family replaces older units for Cooper D/SD with common-rail injection
N14 twin-scroll turbo petrol debuts for Cooper S featuring Valvetronic and direct fuel injection
Updated gearbox software and revised suspension geometry for improved straight-line stability
knownFor: Peak UK registration years now driving the highest volume of replacement enquiries across the model range
N14 timing chain tensioner failures cause catastrophic "death rattle" and valve damage between 60k-90k miles
N47 diesel offers strong mid-range torque but shares the same rear-chain lubrication vulnerability
Pre-facelift variants generate intense specialist rebuild demand for upgraded timing kits and reinforced gaskets
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2010 Mini Convertible replacement engines

Mini Convertible 2010 N47 diesel engine rebuild
Pre-LCI Cooper S engine supply and fit UK

years.preview: R57 LCI N18 Refinement & N16 Base Update
years.description: The mid-cycle LCI facelift swapped the problematic N14 for the revised N18 turbo and introduced the N16 naturally aspirated unit, targeting improved reliability and emissions compliance.
keyChanges: Engine management overhaul with revised turbo oil feed routing and reinforced timing chain guides for N18 series
N16 replaces N12 base engine with updated piston ring profiles and improved PCV ventilation system
Cosmetic interior refresh, LED daytime running lights and updated infotainment across all Convertible trims
2015 marks end of R57 production with strong residual demand for late-model LCI-spec rebuilt units
mainEngines: N18B16C - 1.6L Turbo Petrol High Output (John Cooper Works)
popularModels: Mini John Cooper Works Convertible LCI
knownFor: N18 reduced timing chain failure rates significantly but still requires tensioner monitoring and VANOS solenoid maintenance
N16 oil consumption improves over N12 but Valvetronic motor and eccentric shaft sensor faults remain above 80k miles
LCI facelift models command higher resale values, making specialist recon units with full warranty coverage highly sought after
Carbon build-up on direct-injection intake valves requires periodic walnut blasting to maintain peak performance

N16 oil consumption fix Mini Convertible

years.description: The all-new F57 shifted to the UKL2 front-wheel-drive platform, introducing modular B38 three-cylinder and B48 four-cylinder engines alongside the refined B47 diesel.
keyChanges: UKL2 platform debut with increased structural rigidity, electric power steering and revised soft-top deployment speed
B38A15A 1.5L three-cylinder turbo replaces older 1.6L NA units as standard Cooper engine
B48A20A 2.0L turbo debuts for Cooper S featuring integrated exhaust manifold and improved thermal management
B47D20A replaces N47 with timing chain relocated to the front of the engine, largely solving rear-chain lubrication issues
mainEngines: B48A20A - 2.0L Petrol Turbo 4-cyl (Cooper S, John Cooper Works)
knownFor: Modern refinement with significantly improved NVH, fuel economy and daily drivability compared to R57
B38 timing chain guides and plastic thermostat housing are primary failure points above 60k miles
B48 direct injection promotes severe carbon accumulation on intake valves without regular walnut blast servicing
B47 front-chain design eliminates N47 tensioner fears but requires AdBlue/DPF system compliance verification during rebuild
High demand for OEM-spec recon units with updated ECU coding and immobiliser pairing

years.preview: F57 LCI, Mild-Hybrid Prep & Emissions Compliance
years.description: The F57 facelift introduced updated engine management, stricter Euro 6d emissions compliance, and hardware tweaks preparing the Convertible for eventual mild-hybrid integration.
keyChanges: Euro 6d-TEMP/6d emissions updates with revised DPF calibration, AdBlue dosing strategies and particulate filters
B48 and B38 receive reinforced oil pumps, updated turbo actuators and improved high-pressure fuel pump seals
Interior tech refresh with digital instrument cluster, wireless charging and advanced driver assistance systems
2022+ models feature standardised safety tech, revised suspension damping and optimised start-stop battery integration
mainEngines: B48A20A - 2.0L Petrol (Cooper S, John Cooper Works)
knownFor: Peak modern reliability with improved cooling system durability and stabilised direct-injection fuel mapping
B48 high-pressure fuel pump failures decrease but still require specialist diagnostic calibration during replacement
B38 balance shaft wear and direct injector coking remain critical maintenance points for smooth low-RPM operation
Strong UK specialist network for late-model supply & fit with full immobiliser pairing and software calibration
Rebuilt units include upgraded alloy cooling components and reinforced timing chains to exceed factory longevity

faq.h2: Mini Convertible Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mini Convertible engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Mini Convertible engine replacement costs typically range from £1,050 to £4,500 for rebuilt units, depending on engine code, variant and condition type. Reconditioned N14 or B48 engines sit at the higher end due to specialist machining requirements, while used W10 units offer budget options. Supply and fit adds £450-£850 to these prices nationwide.
keyPoints: Rebuilt N14 engines cost £1,500-£3,500 supply only for R57 Cooper S models
B47 diesel replacements range £1,800-£4,000 depending on AdBlue system compatibility
Used engines from £450-£1,150 offer budget solutions with verified mileage history
Supply and fit services add £450-£850 to engine price across UK specialists
All EngineMarket quotes include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty coverage
items.cta: Compare Mini Convertible engine replacement prices from UK specialists now

items.question: Is the N14 engine in my Mini Convertible really as unreliable as people say?
items.answer: Yes, the N14B16A turbo petrol in 2007-2010 Mini Convertible Cooper S models suffers widespread timing chain tensioner failures between 60k-90k miles. However, rebuilt N14 engines from EngineMarket specialists include upgraded tensioners and guides as standard, eliminating the original weakness while retaining the engine's strong performance characteristics.
keyPoints: N14 timing chain "death rattle" indicates imminent tensioner failure requiring urgent attention
Rebuilt units feature reinforced guides and hydraulic tensioners resolving the original design flaw
Carbon build-up on intake valves requires periodic walnut blasting for optimal N14 performance
Early detection of rattling noise can prevent catastrophic valve damage and costly repairs
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt N14 Mini Convertible engines with upgraded timing components

items.question: Which Mini Convertible engine is the most reliable to buy or replace?
items.answer: The B47D20A diesel and B48A20A petrol engines in F57 Mini Convertibles (2016+) are currently the most reliable options, featuring front-mounted timing chains and improved cooling systems. For older models, the naturally aspirated W10B16A in R52 Coopers offers proven simplicity with fewer failure points than turbocharged variants.
keyPoints: B47 diesel engines relocated timing chains to the front, solving N47 rear-chain lubrication issues
B48 petrol units feature improved thermal management reducing overheating and head gasket risks
W10 naturally aspirated engines avoid turbo and direct injection complexity for lower maintenance costs
N18 revised turbo petrol offers better reliability than N14 but still requires VANOS maintenance
items.cta: Compare reliable Mini Convertible engine options with 12-month warranty coverage today

items.question: How do I find the exact engine code in my Mini Convertible?
items.answer: Check your Mini Convertible's engine code on the VIN plate under the bonnet, in the service book, or by entering your registration on the DVLA website. Common codes include N14B16A for R57 Cooper S, B47D20A for F57 Cooper D, and W11B16A for supercharged R52 models.
keyPoints: Engine codes are stamped on the block near the oil filter housing or timing cover
DVLA online vehicle enquiry confirms engine size and fuel type using your registration number
Specialist suppliers require exact engine codes to ensure bolt-pattern and ECU compatibility
R57 models use N-series codes while F57 models feature B-series engine architecture
items.cta: Enter your registration to identify your Mini Convertible engine code instantly

items.question: What's the difference between reconditioned, rebuilt and used Mini Convertible engines?
items.answer: Used Mini Convertible engines are low-mileage donor units tested for compression; reconditioned units undergo partial disassembly with worn seals and timing components replaced; rebuilt engines receive complete teardown with new pistons, bearings and machining to factory tolerances. Rebuilt offers the highest reliability, used the lowest cost.
keyPoints: Used engines cost £450-£1,150 but carry higher risk without comprehensive warranty coverage
Reconditioned units replace gaskets, seals and timing components for balanced reliability and value
Rebuilt engines include new rotating assemblies and machining for near-factory specification performance
All EngineMarket options include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty for peace of mind
items.cta: Compare reconditioned, rebuilt and used Mini Convertible engine prices side by side

items.question: How long should a Mini Convertible engine last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: Well-maintained Mini Convertible engines typically reach 120,000-150,000 miles before requiring major work, though N14 turbo petrol units often need timing chain attention by 80,000 miles. Regular oil changes with correct BMW-spec lubricants and timely servicing significantly extend lifespan across all engine families.
keyPoints: B47 diesel and B48 petrol F57 engines commonly exceed 150,000 miles with documented service history
N14 and N18 turbo petrol engines require timing chain monitoring from 60,000 miles onwards
Using BMW Longlife-01 or LL-04 spec oil prevents premature wear in Mini Convertible engines
Neglecting coolant changes accelerates thermostat housing and water pump failures across all generations
items.cta: Find rebuilt Mini Convertible engines designed to exceed original factory lifespan specifications

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Mini Convertible or should I scrap it?
items.answer: Replacing a Mini Convertible engine is usually worthwhile if the chassis, bodywork and interior are in good condition and the car's market value exceeds £3,000-£4,000. Rebuilt engines from £1,050-£4,500 often cost less than dealer replacements, extending your Convertible's life affordably.
keyPoints: R57 Cooper S models retain strong residual values justifying engine replacement investment
Supply and fit services from £450-£850 make specialist replacement more affordable than dealers
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components often outperform original factory specification reliability
Scrapping typically returns £200-£500 versus £2,000-£4,000+ value after engine replacement
items.cta: Get a tailored Mini Convertible engine replacement quote to compare against scrap value

items.question: Why does my Mini Convertible engine burn oil and is it fixable?
items.answer: Mini Convertible N12, N16 and N18 petrol engines commonly consume oil due to piston ring design and PCV system wear, typically becoming noticeable above 70,000 miles. Rebuilt engines address this with updated ring profiles and revised ventilation components as standard, eliminating excessive consumption.
keyPoints: N16 and N18 engines benefit from revised piston rings reducing oil burn in rebuilt units
PCV valve failure accelerates oil consumption and should be checked during diagnostic assessment
Using correct BMW-spec oil viscosity prevents accelerated wear in high-mileage Mini Convertible engines
Rebuilt engines include upgraded PCV systems and ring sets resolving original consumption issues
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Mini Convertible engines with upgraded oil control components today

Nationwide network covers all UK postcodes with typical turnaround of 2-4 working days
Specialists perform post-fitment diagnostics ensuring ECU communication and fault code clearance
